bioHotLightweight Curation — AI selection & significance rating
← bioHot

Biological Databases & Knowledge Graphs

New database releases, knowledge graph construction, ontology development, and FAIR data infrastructure.

20 papers curated · High: 2 · Medium: 18 · Low: 0 · Updated: 2026-07-10

bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

A deep-learning-informed prior and Bayesian model for differential AP-MS interactome analysis

This paper presents BayesInteractomics, a new computational framework that combines deep learning with Bayesian statistics to improve the analysis of affinity-purification mass spectrometry (AP-MS) data for mapping protein-protein interactions. By using a neural network trained on protein structures to predict direct binding and incorporating this as prior knowledge, the method outperforms existing tools like SAINTexpress and CompPASS in distinguishing genuine interactors from background noise.

proteomicsmachine-learningtool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

scJET: Full-gene Space Single-cell Expression Generation with Patch-based Transformer Modeling

scJET is a new generative model for single-cell RNA-seq data that works with all genes (full-gene space) instead of just a subset of highly variable genes. It uses a patch-based Transformer to denoise data while preserving both global and local biological structure, providing a more complete and efficient way to generate transcriptome-wide single-cell expression matrices.

single-celltranscriptomicsdeep-learning
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

SPECTER-Based Semantic Triage of Biomedical Literature for Systematic Reviews in Mutational Signature Analysis

This paper presents a reproducible pipeline that uses SPECTER scientific-document embeddings and similarity scoring to automatically rank biomedical literature for systematic reviews, specifically for mutational signature analysis. The method outperforms keyword-only and TF-IDF approaches in screening efficiency, reducing manual review burden while maintaining high recall.

machine-learningtoolmethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

BBBP_Atlas: Unified Interpretable Modeling of Blood Brain Barrier Permeability across Small Molecules and Peptides

The BBBP_Atlas is a new computational resource that unifies fragmented blood-brain barrier permeability data and provides interpretable models for both small molecules and peptides. It addresses key limitations of existing models by using a standardized dataset with controlled redundancy and a unified modeling approach that works across chemically diverse modalities.

drug-discoverymachine-learningdatabase
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Mutational bias shapes protein evolution across RNA viruses

This study demonstrates that mutational biases, rather than natural selection alone, play a major role in shaping protein evolution in RNA viruses. Using SARS-CoV-2 as a model, the authors show that the virus's mutational spectrum explains over 50% of observed amino acid substitutions and predicts proteome-wide composition changes during the pandemic. The findings suggest that neutral evolutionary processes driven by mutation are a key force in viral protein evolution.

evolutiongenomicsmethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Metabolic Rewiring in Triple-Negative Breast Cancer: Systems Analysis of TCGA-BRCA Transcriptome Reveals Prognostic Hub Genes

This study uses a systems biology approach to analyze TCGA-BRCA transcriptome data from triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC) patients, identifying 764 differentially expressed genes and key metabolic hub genes. The authors highlight ATP1A2 as a significant prognostic marker, revealing coordinated metabolic rewiring that could inform future therapeutic strategies for this aggressive cancer subtype.

transcriptomicssystems-biologycancer
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Learning the spatial cell-cell communication network to decode multi-channel signaling and predict network-hub vulnerabilities with MOSANIC

MOSANIC is a new computational tool that learns spatial cell-cell communication networks from multi-channel imaging data, enabling the identification of network hubs and prediction of vulnerabilities to node loss. It decodes how signals propagate across cells and how the entire network responds to perturbations, offering insights into tissue biology and disease intervention points.

spatial-omicsnetwork-analysistool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Assessing tensor decomposition quality of immune profiling data from a dictionary learning perspective

This paper introduces new metrics for assessing the quality of tensor decompositions applied to immune profiling data, drawing inspiration from the experimental data model. The authors demonstrate that these metrics can guide the selection of rank and trial for Non-negative CANDECOMP/PARAFAC (NCPD) decomposition, and show how framing results through dictionary learning provides additional insights.

methodologymachine-learningimmunology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Generative AI Models Reveal Dynamic Views of Aging (DyViA) Phenotypes in Healthy Individuals

This study introduces DyViA, a generative AI platform that uses diffusion and GAN models to predict personalized aging trajectories for healthy individuals. The platform can forecast how specific phenotypes (e.g., physical or cognitive traits) might change with age, offering a new tool for studying healthy aging.

machine-learningdeep-learningaging
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

A five-dimensional functional state space for fingerprinting disease transcriptomes

The authors developed a five-dimensional functional state space that maps transcriptomic signals onto coordinated activity patterns across major biological systems, enabling systematic comparison of disease transcriptomes. This framework provides a reproducible pipeline for functional scoring and cross-condition analysis, moving beyond fragmented gene and pathway lists.

transcriptomicsmethodologysystems-biology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Gene-specific exponent-corrected normalization for library size in bulk RNA-seq

This paper identifies that standard library size normalization methods for bulk RNA-seq often leave residual correlations between normalized counts and sequencing depth in large datasets. The authors propose a gene-specific exponent correction to better remove this technical artifact, improving differential expression analysis.

transcriptomicsmethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) High

IgGM2: An All-Atom Foundation Model for Adaptive Immune Receptor Design

IgGM2 is a new AI model that can simultaneously predict the 3D structure and design the sequences of antibodies, nanobodies, and T-cell receptors, overcoming limitations of previous methods that handled these tasks separately. This unified approach enables more accurate immune receptor design by modeling the coupled variation of sequence, structure, and target binding.

machine-learningprotein-structureimmunology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Gene Program Negotiation Defines Cellular Identity in Single-Cell Transcriptomes

This paper introduces Gene Program Negotiation (GPN), a graph-based computational framework that models how multiple simultaneously active gene programs interact to define cellular identity in single-cell transcriptomes, overcoming limitations of existing methods that analyze programs independently. GPN reconstructs cell-specific regulatory decisions by modeling negotiation among concurrent biological programs.

single-celltranscriptomicsmethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Rectangle: robust and scalable multiscale deconvolution informed by single-cell RNA sequencing data

Rectangle is a new Python framework for deconvolving bulk RNA-seq data using single-cell references. It improves resolution of closely related cell types, scales to large datasets, and accounts for unknown cellular content not present in the reference.

transcriptomicssingle-celltool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Mind the Alignment Gap: A Spatial Transcriptomics Benchmark for Scientific Coding Agents

This paper introduces a framework for automatically building benchmarks to evaluate scientific coding agents, using spatial transcriptomics alignment as a case study. The authors construct 40 tasks from peer-reviewed papers where agents must produce coordinate tables, addressing the challenge of verifying ambiguous outputs in scientific research tasks.

spatial-omicsmachine-learningtool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Coding agents author interpretable single-cell embedding models from the literature

This paper introduces a method where coding agents automatically generate interpretable single-cell embedding models by extracting marker-gene programs directly from the literature, rather than learning dense dimensions de novo. This approach produces sparse, named dimensions that require less post-hoc interpretation and batch correction, improving the biological relevance of embeddings.

single-cellmachine-learningtool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

LeafRank: A phylodynamic framework for inferring relative fitness from single-cell phylogenies in chromosomally unstable tumors

LeafRank is a new computational method that uses single-cell DNA sequencing data to infer the relative growth advantage (fitness) of individual cancer cells, even in complex tumors with extra chromosomes and instability. By modeling how cell lineages branch and grow over time, it can identify which cells are most likely to drive tumor evolution. This approach helps quantify hidden fitness differences that are difficult to measure with existing tools.

cancersingle-celltool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) High

EZSolver: Template-free prediction of polar enzymatic mechanisms via bidirectional flow matching and search

EZSolver is a new template-free, generative AI framework that predicts polar enzymatic reaction mechanisms without relying on predefined rule-based dictionaries. It uses a flow matching model and bidirectional beam search to learn chemistry directly, enabling it to generalize to novel reactions that traditional methods cannot handle.

machine-learningmethodologystructural-biology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

BertST: BERT-based Spatial Domain Identification in Patient Data

This paper introduces BertST, a BERT-based model for identifying spatial domains in spatial transcriptomics data. Unlike existing GNN-based methods, BertST leverages a Transformer architecture to jointly model local and global gene expression patterns with spatial information, improving domain identification accuracy on patient data.

transcriptomicsspatial-omicsdeep-learning
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Thematic Shifts in Early-High-Impact Cancer Genomics and Diagnostics Research: A Bibliometric and Semantic Analysis

This study uses a bibliometric framework and large language models to identify the most influential recent publications in cancer genomics and diagnostics, revealing thematic shifts in topics that attract early citation prominence. The findings can inform research strategy and investment decisions by highlighting which areas of cancer genomics are gaining traction.

cancermachine-learningmethodology
